macro_segments.title: Scriptural derivation of kefel liability through Klal U-Prat structure and Rabbi Yishmael's hermeneutics

micro_segments.title: Opening context: finishing the drashot on kefel for all objects from two Torah passages
The two main passages on theft: Ki Yignov (direct case of ganav) and If Found (shomer context)
Why the passage states the list of animals (shor, chamor, seh) and chooses Mishor ad Seh Chayim
Gemara's question: why not just say Gneiva and Shor to create klal u-prat?
The assumption of Rabbi Yishmael: klal u-prat u-klal as interpretive principle with multiple interpretations
Chayim as final klal and Himatzei Timatzei as critical expansion term
Two Kelalim (Himatzei and Timatzei) creating Klal U-Prat U-Klal with animals as pratim
Shedi Shor between Himatzei and Timatzei to expand scope of gneiva to all chattel
Davar Hametaltel V'Gufo Mammon as the abstraction level for what kefel applies to

macro_segments.title: Exclusions from kefel and the Ribui Approach: land, slaves, documents, and davar mesuyam

micro_segments.title: Ribui approach as alternative to Klal U-Prat U-Klal: Himatzei Timatzei as ribui with mi'utim
Three mi'utim (exclusions): Karka, Avadim, and Shtarot; gneiva and chayim remain undefined
Achyia L'Keren K'Ein Sheganav: restoring stolen object to state when taken

macro_segments.title: Dispute whether both clauses concern actual ganav or both concern to'ein ta'anat ganav

micro_segments.title: Option 1: One clause teaches ganav himself, other teaches to'ein ta'anat ganav claiming theft
Option 2: Both clauses teach to'ein ta'anat ganav; Himatzei Timatzei teaches scope of kefel
Rava bar Ahilai: modeh bi-knas patur—one who admits a fine does not pay even after witnesses
Deriving exemption from admission via Asher Yarshi'un: judges must find guilty, not confession
Tanna of Rabbi Yishmael: parsha repeated only for new chidush, not every detail
Abiyi's refutation: Torah would not need to state kefel by ganav if only shvu'a required it

macro_segments.title: Possession and domain: B'Yado means any form of possession, not literal hand

micro_segments.title: Ein Li Ella Yado: initially only literal hand is mentioned; Himatzei Timatzei expands possession
Gago, Chatzeiro, V'Kapifo: yard and enclosure also count as taking possession
Himatzei Timatzei serves dual purpose: possession method and scope of liable objects

macro_segments.title: Principle of restoration: Keren according to time of theft; kefel and arba'ah v'chamishah by time of trial

micro_segments.title: Rav's statement: Keren k'ein sheganav—principal based on value when stolen
Kefel and Arba'ah V'Chamishah k'sha'at Ha'amadah B'Din: fines by court standing value
Rav Sheshet's challenge: braita states Chushah V'Hashminah—thinning or fattening returns to original value
Exception when ganav adds value: Amrei Mishum—ganav cannot demand payment for his own additions
Symmetry of loss: Thinner animal also reverts to original value; neither ganav gains nor loses
Raba's barrel of wine case: increased market value vs. active destruction by ganav create different obligations
Active destruction: if ganav drinks wine worth four instead of one, principal assessed at four
Breakdown of liability: original act (principal), active new damage (higher value), passive breakage (original value)
Ganav vs. shomer: ganav pays for original theft regardless of negligence; shomer liable for negligence not at time of taking
Conclusion: principal from theft time; kefel from trial time; active damage raises principal; passive changes do not

topical_tags.term: Kefel (double payment)
Klal U-Prat U-Klal (general-particular-general)
Gneiva/Gneivah (theft/burglary)
To'ein Ta'anat Ganav (claiming theft occurred)
Shor, Chamor, Seh (ox, donkey, sheep as examples)
Chayim (living things; animate requirement)
Himatzei Timatzei (if found; dual verb form)
Karka (land; excluded from kefel)
Shtarot (documents; excluded from kefel)
Avadim (slaves; excluded from kefel)
Davar Hametaltel V'Gufo Mammon (chattel with intrinsic value)
Ribui (inclusive expansion; broad interpretation)
Modeh Bi-Knas Patur (one who admits a fine is exempt)
Achyia L'Keren K'Ein Sheganav (restore principal to state when stolen)
Keren (principal; original amount stolen)
Arba'ah V'Chamishah (four and five times payment if slaughtered/sold)
Sha'at Ha'amadah B'Din (time of court standing; time fines assessed)
Sha'at Hagneivah (time of theft; time principal assessed)
B'Yado (in his hand/possession; scope of ganav liability)
Shomer (watchman; steward with different liability rules)
Shvu'ah (oath/swearing)
Rabbi Yishmael (named authority on hermeneutic rules)
Rav (Abaye's discussion partner; states principal rule)
Rav Sheshet (disputes Rav's statement on fattening/thinning)
Raba/Rava Bar Ahilai (authority on modeh bi-knas; wine case)
Chushah V'Hashminah (thinning and fattening animal cases)
Davar Mesuyam (specific/defined thing; unclear exclusion)
